title: Voice driven modification of sub-parts of assets in computer simulations
abstract: A computer simulation object such as a chair is described by voice or photo input to render a 2D image. Machine learning may be used to convert voice input to the 2D image. The 2D image is converted to a 3D asset and the 3D asset or portions thereof are used in the computer simulation, such as a computer game, as the object such as a chair.
claims: 1. A method, comprising: receiving at least one two dimensional (2D) image of a computer simulation object; converting the 2D image to a three dimensional (3D) asset; using at least one neural network, altering a first portion of the 3D asset and not altering a second portion of the 3D asset to create an altered asset; blending the first and second portions of the object together at least in part by changing pixel weights of pixels of at least one of the portions; and presenting the altered asset in at least one computer simulation, wherein the altering comprises one or more of altering a color of the first portion, a style of the first portion, a texture of a surface of the first portion.
